AniOS · canonical suite · 9 diagrams synchronized

Architecture after multimodal retrieval

Six of the nine canonical diagrams changed in this pass. Each is rendered below from its authoritative .mmd source, with the scope it owns and what moved. The three unchanged views — memory overview, tool memory, and architecture maintenance — were not touched, so their relationships still hold.

Relevant queries
14 / 14correct top-1 match
Distractor queries
4 / 4correctly returned nothing
Distance ceiling
0.96coarse filter
Runner-up margin
0.015the real discriminator
Backend tests
367ruff · black · mypy strict
Full system Runtime & deployment Chat orchestration Search & research Visual artifacts Memory Frontend

Every implemented component and its trust boundaries

Full system

changedAdded the isolated Google research worker, Tavily fallback, and non-content quota store.

Processes, ports, protocols, Compose topology

Runtime & deployment

changedAdded Google Search as an opt-in external worker and a durable provider-count volume.

Routing, memory planning, streaming, SSE contract

Chat orchestration

changedGuarded search now selects Google, Tavily fallback, or explicit dual-provider verification.

Privacy, provider policy, cloud-worker isolation, quota, provenance

Search & research

changedAdded a Google ADK research subagent with Tavily fallback and deliberate cross-check mode.

Generation, upload, storage, vision analysis, retrieval

Visual artifacts

changedImages are embedded at store time; ImageRetrievalPolicy applies a distance ceiling plus a runner-up margin.

Every memory form, write authority, retrieval, lifecycle

Memory

changedVision analysis text is indexed under its own purpose, kept out of the approval-gated user-fact path.

Client state, components, typed API and SSE client

Frontend

changedSource cards now identify which research provider supplied each result.

Sources live in docs/diagrams/*.mmd and are the authority; the checked-in SVGs and this page are both renderings of them. npm run docs:diagram:check verifies every registered pair.
